questions.question: Explain, with an example, how a blockchain achieves immutability.
keyPoints: Definition of immutability in this context.
Concept of linking blocks via previous hash.
Consequence of tampering (e.g., need to re-mine all subsequent blocks).
Simple example (e.g., altering a transaction in block 101).

questions.question: Compare and contrast a public blockchain and a private blockchain. Discuss their ideal use cases, two advantages, and two disadvantages of each.
keyPoints: Definition of Public Blockchain (open, decentralized, permissionless).
Definition of Private Blockchain (restricted, centralized control, permissioned).
Use Case for Private: Enterprise supply chain (e.g., Walmart).
Disadvantages of Public: Slow, less private.
Advantages of Private: Efficient, scalable, compliant.
Disadvantages of Private: Less decentralized, requires trust in operator.
